text:
Once upon a time in a curious town, there was a black cat named Whiskers. Whiskers had bright orange fur and an unruly mane that always looked like it was holding a tiny bowtie. He lived under the hood of the oldest oak tree in the park, and his favorite spot offered him a superb view of the world.

Every morning, Whiskers would leisurely stretch out his legs and yawn, his amber eyes glinting with mischief. He loved to watch the humans pass by, particularly the children playing tag in the garden. They would throw seeds and fruits to him, and he'd chase them around with a grin.

There was one little girl named Clara. Her laugh was as sweet as strawberries. Clara adored the cat—she even named him after her late grandmother. She would often sneak seeds from her lunch into his food, ensuring he had enough to gnaw on.
